results.notes: 2 clicks + fill from queue to saved ticket; required fields (Client*, Subject*) signposted with asterisks before submit. T-18 created with subject/description/client/priority all persisted; appears in the queue.

Resolution capture works and persists via the Note/Reply path. psa-3vzk (bug/high): the dedicated 'Change Status' panel silently no-ops (submits an empty body that the notes endpoint requires). psa-j8dh (ux/med): the prominent green 'Resolve' button captures no resolution and never prompts — and (confirmed in TicketObserver) leaves resolution empty so NO mining is triggered.

Walked New->In Progress->Pending Client->Resolved->Closed (and reopens). Each status persists, shows on the ticket and in the list, and records a consistent 'from -> to' audit note with a 'Status changed to X' banner. NOTE: the 'Change Status' control itself is broken (shared with psa-3vzk); transitions were verified via the Note panel and the Resolve/Close quick buttons.

Reopen works: ticket returns to an active status, is editable, and the prior resolution is PRESERVED. psa-9yia (ux/low): there is no labeled 'Reopen' control (header shows only 'Close' on a resolved ticket); reopening requires the Note/Reply panel, and the natural 'Change Status' route is broken (psa-3vzk).

Disabled: /wiki and /clients/{id}/wiki return 404 and the client-page Wiki tab is cleanly hidden (not a dead link). Enabled: global index and client Wiki tab reachable. The master switch and its mining-toggle dependency are clearly labeled in Settings, with cost transparency ($2-8/day).

Client -> Wiki is 1 click; the index lists all pages with search as the top affordance; search returns relevant pages. psa-7ph7 (ux/med): individual wiki pages are navigational dead-ends — no link back to the index, no sibling-page links, no in-page search, and a plain-text (non-link) breadcrumb; reading network/infra/m365 forces browser-Back through the index.

Resolving fact-rich T-18 (auto-mine on, queue worker running) mined 1 accurate, consolidated fact onto infrastructure/assets, born 'unverified', with source_refs ticket#18. Provenance panel renders counts/badges/source refs; the 'Unverified' badge is color+TEXT (accessible). psa-ux48 (ux/low): the provenance panel crams the fact text into a ~1-2-word-per-line column. Observation: an OLDER pre-session run (WikiRun #1, ticket#4) showed facts_extracted=0 / discarded=3 with no discarded_details, but a later re-mine (#2) extracted 5 — the system self-corrected and newer runs expose discarded_details.

Confirm = 1 click (status -> confirmed, confirmed_by set). Correct = open inline editor, edit, Save: supersedes (original retired with superseded_by set; a new confirmed fact carries the corrected wording). Retire = Retire -> Yes (lightweight confirm) -> retired. All statuses update; actions are cheap (1-2 clicks). The actions live in the cramped provenance panel (psa-ux48).

findings.title: Change Status panel silently fails — ticket status never changes
Resolve button captures no resolution and never prompts for one
No discoverable 'Reopen' action on resolved/closed tickets
Wiki pages are navigational dead-ends — no link to index, siblings, or search
Provenance panel crams fact text into a narrow column (1-2 words/line)

harness_notes: The QA Playwright runner (scripts/qa/playwright-runner.mjs) had two tooling gaps that blocked driving the product: (1) the 'snapshot' action used page.accessibility.snapshot(), removed in modern Playwright (100% broken) — repaired to page.locator('body').ariaSnapshot(); (2) no 'selectOption' action, so native <select> controls (every create/resolve form) could not be driven — added a selectOption action. These are TEST-TOOLING fixes (not product changes); product findings are unaffected. Surfaced for the harness maintainer to review/keep. Also note: harness 'click' does not await navigation, so each form submit must be the last meaningful action and be followed by a 'screenshot' to let the POST/redirect settle (chaining submit->interact races and silently drops the POST).
